text: Transverse perineal support operation for perineal descent Video vignette

We present the case of a fifty-five years old female referred to our unit for obstructive defecation symptoms with an ODS score of twenty-two. Clinical evaluation revealed a weak perineal body with a four centimeter descending perineum during straining attempt. Proctoscopy performed in clinic was positive for occult rectal prolapse. Colonoscopy was negative.
Edification proctography diagnosed a rectal prolapse with incomplete rectal clearance and descending perineum.

The patient is positioned in lithotomy under spinal anesthesia. The surgeon performs an internal Delorme procedure, following standard technique, with visible administration of antithrombotic and antibiotic prophylaxis.
The patient is prepped and draped in a sterile manner. The surgeon digitally evaluates the perineal weakness before making bilateral perineal incisions at the ischial tuberosities.

The surgeon performs dissection anterior to the transverse perineal muscle using a blunt trochar. An O-proline suture is passed through, and a tailored biological mesh is positioned and secured to aponeurotic bony structures with two O-PDS sutures.
The surgeon closes the skin using trio-vicryl rapid interrupted sutures, ensuring the perineal area is resistant and strong through digital examination.

Postoperative course was characterized by mild pain and hematoma formation at the level of perineal incisions. Pain was well controlled with intravenous acetaminophen, one grams every eight hours, and hematoma was treated by topic therapy.
Patient was discharged on the fourth post-operative day. Follow-up was scheduled at six months and one year.

A digital evaluation of perineum strengthness and a dynamic evaluation during straining was performed. We didn't record any mesh-related complications. Patient didn't report any sexual or voiding dysfunctions, while obstructive symptoms were still improved at one-year follow-up. ODS score was eight and eleven, respectively, at six months and one year.
